name: OpenCode
description: OpenCode is a free, open-source AI coding agent for the terminal. MIT-licensed and BYOK (bring your own key) — connects to any OpenAI-compatible API including Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, and local models. No subscription required.

provenance.notes: OpenCode is MIT-licensed open-source software. Free to use; cost depends on which LLM API keys you supply. No subscription or pricing page — cost is entirely pass-through to your chosen model provider.
OpenCode is model-agnostic. Context length, quality, and benchmarks depend entirely on the model API you configure (e.g. Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o, Gemini, or a local Ollama model).

pricing.notes: Free and open-source (MIT). You pay only for the LLM API keys you configure — OpenCode itself is always $0.

provenance.notes: MIT license confirmed at github.com/sst/opencode. No pricing model for the tool itself.

models.notes: Bring your own API key — Claude, GPT-4o, Gemini, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible provider.

usage_limits.notes: No usage limits imposed by OpenCode — limits are set by your chosen LLM provider and API key quota.

features.notes: Terminal-native agentic coding agent. Supports file edits, shell commands, multi-step reasoning. Context window depends on your configured model. No built-in IDE integration — runs in any terminal.
